figures.caption: Fig. 3: Qualitative results in simulation. Vision-only baselines often exhibit artifacts (e.g ., holes or inconsistent relative dimensions) under occlusion. By leveraging contact cues, our method produces more physically plausible reconstructions.
Table 2: Pose estimation results in simulation.

Fig. 2: Training procedure and Architecture. a1): We train a Structure-VAE autoencoder that reconstructs pose-aware object SDFs. a2): Using the frozen VAE encoder, we build latent datasets and train a conditional flow transformer from scratch using pose-consistent, unoccluded object images. a3): We finetune the Structureflow on occluded manipulation scenes, conditioning on visible RGB evidence, occluder/visibility masks, hand latent, and tactile features. b) The architecture of the Flow Transformer. Figure design inspired by [66].
